chunks.text: Degraded performance for some project shares | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Degraded performance for some project shares Our Network Appliance filer in Sutardja Dai Hall has been experiencing performanc

Sutardja Dai Hall has been experiencing performance issues since late afternoon Thursday. Chiefly affected are users of project shares on cluster.eecs or the Instructional group’s inst-fs.eecs. Staff are working to restore service. UPDATE [2014-07-08

are working to restore service. UPDATE [2014-07-08 16:28:26 | Lars Rohrbach] Service for cluster.eecs and inst-fs.eecs is back to normal. The NetApp filer in SDH that manages backup snapshots of production storage failed over to its High Availability

ction storage failed over to its High Availability partner last Thursday afternoon, just before the long weekend, due to a failure of the in-room UPS system during a systems self-test. The monthly backup tape dumps (which take several days to finish)

kup tape dumps (which take several days to finish) were restarted on the cluster partner, causing it to experience unusually high load for several days, which interfered with NFS automounts. After the tape backups finished, several other ongoing snap

tape backups finished, several other ongoing snapshot transfers prevented the systems from immediately reverting to normal mode. Both systems have now been switched back to their primary service mode. We are in the process of repairing/refitting th

e. We are in the process of repairing/refitting the UPS to prevent similar problems in the future. Resolved as of 2014-07-08 13:30:00 Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Statu
d 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
